judgements.title: Spring Meadows Hospital v. Harjol Ahluwalia
judgements.summary: Medical negligence by doctor/hospital amounts to deficiency in service under Consumer Protection Act. Parents can file as consumers for treatment given to their minor child. Hospital ordered to pay ₹5 lakh compensation.
judgements.key_principle: Medical negligence is a deficiency in service. Parents/guardians can file on behalf of minor patients.

judgements.title: Indian Medical Association v. V.P. Shantha
judgements.summary: Landmark ruling: Medical services are 'services' under the Consumer Protection Act. Doctors providing services for consideration can be sued for deficiency. Free services at government hospitals were initially excluded.
judgements.key_principle: Paid medical services fall under COPRA. Doctors and hospitals are liable for deficiency of service.

judgements.title: Lucknow Development Authority v. M.K. Gupta
judgements.summary: Government/statutory bodies providing housing services are covered under Consumer Protection Act. Delay in handing over possession of flats amounts to deficiency in service. LDA ordered to pay compensation and interest.
judgements.key_principle: Government bodies providing housing are 'service providers'. Delay in possession is deficiency of service.

judgements.title: Ghaziabad Development Authority v. Balbir Singh
judgements.summary: Consumer Forums can award compensation for mental agony and harassment in addition to refund of amount. GDA directed to refund principal amount along with 18% interest and compensation for mental harassment.
judgements.key_principle: Compensation for mental agony, harassment and inconvenience is an independent head of relief.

judgements.title: Maruti Udyog Ltd. v. Susheel Kumar Gabgotra
judgements.summary: Manufacturer is liable for defects in vehicle even if discovered after warranty period expires, if the defect was latent (hidden). Consumer can seek replacement or refund of defective vehicle.
judgements.key_principle: Manufacturer liable for latent defects even post-warranty. Replacement/refund is valid remedy.

judgements.title: Ambrish Kumar Shukla v. Ferrous Infrastructure Pvt. Ltd.
judgements.summary: Landmark NCDRC ruling: Allottees can approach Consumer Commission even for RERA registered projects. Builder cannot use RERA as a bar to consumer jurisdiction. Compensation for delay in possession at SBI MCLR + 2%.
judgements.key_principle: RERA and Consumer Protection Act operate simultaneously. Allottees have choice of forum.

judgements.title: State Bank of India v. Om Prakash
judgements.summary: Bank's failure to credit interest on fixed deposit and misappropriation of funds amounts to deficiency in service. Bank ordered to pay principal, interest, and additional compensation of ₹50,000 for mental agony.
judgements.key_principle: Banks are service providers. Errors in accounts, FDs and mismanagement of funds is deficiency of service.

judgements.title: New India Assurance Co. Ltd. v. Nusli Neville Wadia
judgements.summary: Insurance company cannot repudiate claim on technical grounds if the insured has complied with all material conditions. Arbitrary rejection of genuine claims amounts to deficiency in service.
judgements.key_principle: Technical repudiation of genuine insurance claims is deficiency of service. Good faith doctrine applies.

judgements.title: Oriental Insurance Co. v. Premlata Shukla
judgements.summary: Health insurance company cannot add exclusion clauses after accepting premium. Pre-existing disease clause must be disclosed clearly at time of policy issue. Undisclosed exclusions are void.
judgements.key_principle: Undisclosed exclusion clauses in insurance policies are not enforceable. Full disclosure mandatory.

judgements.title: v/s Amazon Seller Services Pvt. Ltd. (Flipkart case)
judgements.summary: E-commerce platform held jointly liable with seller for delivery of counterfeit/substandard goods. Platform cannot escape liability by claiming to be mere marketplace. Consumer awarded full refund plus ₹15,000 compensation.
judgements.key_principle: E-commerce platforms share liability with sellers for counterfeit goods. Marketplace defense rejected.

judgements.title: MakeMyTrip India Pvt. Ltd. v. Vikas Gupta
judgements.summary: Online travel portal's failure to refund cancelled flight tickets within stipulated time, and charging hidden fees, amounts to deficiency of service and unfair trade practice. Full refund plus compensation ordered.
judgements.key_principle: Online travel portals are liable for refund delays and hidden charges. These constitute unfair trade practices.

judgements.title: Nizam's Institute of Medical Sciences v. Prasanth S. Dhananka
judgements.summary: Hospital negligence leading to permanent disability entitles complainant to compensation for future medical expenses, loss of earning capacity, and pain and suffering. Supreme Court awarded over ₹1 crore in compensation.
judgements.key_principle: Compensation for medical negligence includes future medical expenses and complete loss of earning capacity.

judgements.title: Trai v. Vodafone India Ltd.
judgements.court: Telecom Disputes Settlement and Appellate Tribunal
judgements.summary: Telecom operator's activation of unsolicited VAS (Value Added Services) and charging for them without explicit consent amounts to unfair trade practice. Full refund and ₹1,000 penalty per incident directed.
judgements.key_principle: Unsolicited VAS activation and billing without consent is unfair trade practice. Explicit opt-in required.

judgements.title: Station Manager, Indian Railways v. Surendra Nath
judgements.summary: The Railways were held liable for deficiency of service due to theft of a passenger's luggage in a reserved coach. It was ruled that the ticket collector failed in his duty to prevent unauthorized entry.
judgements.key_principle: Railways are liable for theft in reserved compartments if railway staff fails to prevent unauthorized entry.

judgements.title: Air India Ltd. v. Geeta Jethani
judgements.summary: Airline held liable for extreme delay in flight departure and loss of baggage without proper compensation or care for passengers. Compensation awarded for mental agony.
judgements.key_principle: Airlines are strictly liable for loss of baggage and extreme flight delays causing passenger distress.

judgements.title: Sehgal School of Competition v. Dalmacia
judgements.summary: Educational coaching institute cannot retain the entire advance fee if a student leaves the course midway. The NCDRC directed a pro-rata refund of fees.
judgements.key_principle: No educational institute can retain full advance fees if a student leaves midway. Pro-rata refund is mandatory.

judgements.title: FIITJEE Ltd. v. Minathi Rath
judgements.summary: Charging lump-sum advance fees for a 2-year course and refusing refund when the student leaves early is an Unfair Trade Practice. Institutions can only charge fees for the period a student attends.
judgements.key_principle: Lump-sum advance fees for multi-year courses are illegal and constitute an Unfair Trade Practice.

judgements.title: HDFC Bank Ltd. v. Balwinder Singh
judgements.summary: Bank held liable for employing goons (recovery agents) who forcibly repossessed a financed vehicle. NCDRC awarded heavy compensation for use of muscle power.
judgements.key_principle: Banks cannot use force or muscle men for loan recovery or vehicle repossession.

judgements.title: ICICI Bank Ltd. v. Prakash Kaur
judgements.summary: Supreme Court strongly condemned the practice of banks employing recovery agents to forcibly take possession of vehicles. It violates the right to life and dignity.
judgements.key_principle: Forcible repossession by banks through recovery agents is illegal and violates constitutional rights.

judgements.title: LIC of India v. Smt. Asha Goel
judgements.summary: Repudiation of life insurance claim must be based on solid evidence of material suppression of facts. Routine rejection of claims without proper investigation is a deficiency in service.
judgements.key_principle: Insurance companies bear the burden to prove deliberate suppression of material facts when rejecting a claim.

judgements.title: Dr. Laxman Balkrishna Joshi v. Dr. Trimbak Bapu Godbole
judgements.summary: A doctor has a duty of care in deciding whether to undertake a case, deciding what treatment to give, and administering the treatment. Breach of these duties constitutes medical negligence.
judgements.key_principle: Establishes the three duties of care owed by a medical practitioner to a patient.

judgements.title: Jacob Mathew v. State of Punjab
judgements.summary: Distinguished between civil negligence and criminal negligence for doctors. Simple lack of care is civil negligence (deficiency of service), while gross negligence invites criminal liability.
judgements.key_principle: Medical negligence under Consumer Act requires showing a breach of ordinary standard of care, distinct from criminal negligence.

judgements.title: V.N. Shrikhande v. Anita Sena Fernandes
judgements.summary: Gauze left inside a patient's abdomen during surgery is a clear case of medical negligence (res ipsa loquitur). Limitation period begins when the patient discovers the foreign object.
judgements.key_principle: Leaving surgical instruments/sponges inside a patient is per se medical negligence.

judgements.title: Experion Developers Pvt. Ltd. v. Sushma Ashok Shiroor
judgements.summary: Supreme Court held that consumer forums can direct builders to refund the entire amount with interest if possession is significantly delayed, rather than just awarding compensation.
judgements.key_principle: Unreasonable delay in possession entitles buyers to seek a full refund with interest.

judgements.title: Pioneer Urban Land and Infrastructure Ltd. v. Govindan Raghavan
judgements.summary: One-sided, heavily biased builder-buyer agreements constitute an unfair trade practice. Buyers are not bound by completely arbitrary terms imposed by the builder.
judgements.key_principle: One-sided builder-buyer agreements are Unfair Trade Practices and non-binding.

judgements.title: Bharti Airtel Ltd. v. A.R. Prabhakar
judgements.summary: Telecom company held liable for arbitrary disconnection of service and arbitrary billing without notice. Compensation awarded for harassment and loss of connectivity.
judgements.key_principle: Arbitrary disconnection and billing by telecom companies is a deficiency of service.

judgements.title: Pooja Daryani v. M/s. Make My Trip
judgements.court: State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission, Delhi
judgements.summary: Booking portal held liable for failing to provide confirmed hotel booking despite receiving payment, leaving the consumer stranded in a foreign country. Heavy compensation for mental agony awarded.
judgements.key_principle: Travel portals are fully liable for confirmed bookings and cannot shift blame solely to the hotel.

judgements.title: SBI Cards & Payment Services Pvt. Ltd. v. Rohidas Jadhav
judgements.summary: Credit card company held liable for continuing to charge annual fees and late fees after the consumer had requested card cancellation. Reversal of charges and compensation ordered.
judgements.key_principle: Billing post-cancellation request of credit cards is an Unfair Trade Practice.

judgements.title: Post Master, Post Office v. Shyamali Das
judgements.summary: Postal department held liable for delay in delivering a speed post article containing interview call letter, resulting in loss of employment opportunity.
judgements.key_principle: Delay in speed post delivery causing tangible loss to consumer is actionable deficiency.

judgements.title: Swarn Singh v. Omaxe Chandigarh Extension Developers Pvt. Ltd.
judgements.summary: NCDRC ruled that builders cannot force allottees to accept alternative properties if the original project is delayed or abandoned. Allottees have an absolute right to a refund.
judgements.key_principle: Builders cannot force alternative flats on buyers; refund is an absolute right upon delay.

judgements.title: National Insurance Co. Ltd. v. Hindustan Safety Glass Works Ltd.
judgements.summary: Insurance companies cannot reject claims citing delay in intimation if the delay was due to unavoidable circumstances and the claim is otherwise genuine.
judgements.key_principle: Genuine insurance claims cannot be rejected merely on grounds of delayed intimation.

judgements.title: C.C.E. v. BSNL
judgements.summary: Primarily a taxation case regarding SIM cards, but established that providing a telephone connection is a service. However, the specific consumer claim was dismissed on technical tax grounds.
judgements.key_principle: Telecom services are recognized as 'services', though taxation rules apply differently.

judgements.title: HDFC Bank Ltd. v. Jesna Jose
judgements.summary: Bank held liable for unauthorized fraudulent transactions on a credit card. The commission emphasized the RBI guidelines that place zero liability on the customer if reported promptly.
judgements.key_principle: Zero liability of consumer for unauthorized banking transactions if reported within 3 days.

judgements.title: Bata India Ltd. v. Dinesh Parshad Raturi
judgements.court: State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission, Chandigarh
judgements.summary: Charging extra for paper carry bags with the brand logo is an unfair trade practice. Stores must provide free carry bags to customers.
judgements.key_principle: Charging customers for branded carry bags constitutes an Unfair Trade Practice.

judgements.title: SpiceJet Ltd. v. Ranju Aery
judgements.summary: Airline held liable for cancelling a confirmed ticket without prior intimation to the passenger. Passenger awarded full refund and compensation for mental agony and missing a scheduled event.
judgements.key_principle: Airlines must provide prior notice for cancellations; failure to do so is actionable deficiency.

judgements.title: Sushma Sharma v. Omaxe Ltd.
judgements.summary: NCDRC ruled that a buyer is not bound to accept possession of an incomplete flat without an Occupancy Certificate (OC). Offering possession without OC is illegal.
judgements.key_principle: Offering possession of a flat without a valid Occupancy Certificate is deficiency of service.

judgements.title: United India Insurance Co. Ltd. v. M/s. Harchand Rai Chandan Lal
judgements.summary: Insurance claim for burglary dismissed because the policy condition specifically required 'visible marks of forced entry', which were absent. Terms of insurance contract must be strictly construed.
judgements.key_principle: Insurance policy terms are to be strictly interpreted. If specific conditions like 'forced entry' aren't met, claim fails.

judgements.title: Big Bazaar (Future Retail Ltd.) v. Ashok Kumar
judgements.summary: NCDRC upheld the lower forum's order directing retail chains to stop charging extra for carry bags. It was held to be a deceptive practice to not disclose this charge before the customer reaches the billing counter.
judgements.key_principle: Undisclosed charges for carry bags at billing counters is an Unfair Trade Practice.

judgements.title: Dr. V.K. Sankaran v. State of Kerala
judgements.summary: Government hospitals providing free treatment do not fall under the Consumer Protection Act unless some patients are charged. However, this established the nuance of when government doctors can be held liable.
judgements.key_principle: Free medical services at govt hospitals are generally outside COPRA, unless charges apply to some.

judgements.title: Amazon Seller Services Pvt. Ltd. v. Vishudanand
judgements.summary: Amazon held liable for a seller delivering a soap bar instead of a mobile phone. The commission ruled that Amazon failed to exercise due diligence in onboarding and monitoring sellers.
judgements.key_principle: E-commerce marketplaces have a duty of care and cannot merely rely on safe harbor when fraud is evident.

judgements.title: Delhi Development Authority v. D.C. Sharma
judgements.summary: DDA held liable for double allotment of the same plot. The NCDRC directed DDA to provide an alternative plot in the same area or pay the current market value of the plot as compensation.
judgements.key_principle: Double allotment of plots is severe deficiency. Compensation must reflect current market value.

judgements.title: Zomato Media Pvt. Ltd. v. Abhigyan
judgements.summary: Food delivery app held liable for failing to deliver food on time and refusing to refund the amount for the un-delivered food. Ordered to refund the amount and pay compensation for mental agony.
judgements.key_principle: Food delivery apps are liable for non-delivery and arbitrary refusal of refunds.

judgements.title: Tata Motors Ltd. v. Antonio Paulo Vaz
judgements.summary: Manufacturer cannot be held liable for a dealer selling an old/used car as new unless there is evidence of the manufacturer's involvement. However, the dealer is strictly liable for the unfair trade practice.
judgements.key_principle: Dealers selling used cars as new commit an Unfair Trade Practice. Manufacturer liability requires proof of collusion.
